title: Must use mountlabel when creating builtin volumes
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
If user modifies the filelabel security-opt, builtin volumes will now use the specified label.
```

comments.body: you need to rebase to fix unrelated CI errors (#17247)
Happy greeen test buttons.  A quick scan and the code LGTM.  But I definitely want a head nod from @Luap99 before merging to make sure his concerns were answered.
@Luap99 I would really like to get this merged, and can look at fixing the order of a kubernetes yaml definition to make sure the label is processed before the volumes are created.
I don't want code that does nothing but confuses readers to be merged, please address my comment.
@Luap99 I removed the code you were complaining about.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Lint's angry about an extra trailing space
Is this safe for volumes being used by more than one container, given that it looks like we're copying the MCS part of the label, too?

As long as the containers are using the same file type, no way to truly deal with containers with different file types.  Unless we add a new type.

I have a goal of adding an option to create a volume with a specific file type.

This would allow us to have a third label shared between two container_file_t types.

Most likely this would not happen often, really only needed for RHIVOS.
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
